title: Unseen Observers
lines: You crouch on the ridge, scanning the verdant basin. The silence is absolute, save for the occasional, wet slap of something moving beneath the glassy surface of the pool.
As you watch, the bioluminescent fronds of the palms seem to pulse in unison, a slow, biological strobe light that reveals half-seen shapes darting between the trunks.
They are small, pale, and move with a fluid, insectile grace. They do not see you yet, but they are numerous.

options.desc: Take a deep breath and decide your next move
